commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0202c000a1702f8a9fc9b854f3efa210a1c06e82)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an in-memory memo for decoded models.dev cache artifacts, invalidates it on save, regenerates the Codex parser hash, and adds focused ModelsDevPricingTests coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: when `modelsDevCatalog` is nil, the row-scanning cost path can call `ModelsDevCache.load` per row, and current main decodes on every call. I did not run a live large-history performance profile in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs contributor real behavior proof and a targeted fix for the unchanged failed-load fallback; automation cannot provide the contributor's runtime evidence.

target.title: Memoize models.dev catalog to fix cost-scan CPU spike
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a CPU spike / menu freeze during token-cost usage refreshes (reported in #1311).

When a cost lookup runs **without** a pre-resolved catalog, `CostUsagePricing.modelsDevLookup` falls back to `ModelsDevPricingPipeline.lookup` → `ModelsDevCache.load`, which reads and JSON-decodes the full **~800 KB** `models.dev` catalog from disk. That fallback is hit **once per usage row** (`codexModeSplitMaps` / `claude` scan loops call `codexCostUSD` / `claudeCostUSD` per row). Whenever the top-level catalog load returns nil for a window — cold start, the 24 h TTL refresh writing the cache mid-scan, or a decode hiccup — the entire scan of a large `~/.codex` history re-decodes the catalog hundreds of thousands of times.

A macOS CPU resource report captured this precisely: **90 s of CPU at 95%** over a 94 s window, with the heaviest stack:

```
UsageStore.refreshTokenUsage
 └ CostUsageFetcher.loadTokenSnapshot
    └ CostUsageScanner.loadCodexDaily
       └ scanCodexFile → rescanCodexFile → codexModeSplitMaps
          └ CostUsagePricing.codexCostUSD
             └ ModelsDevCache.load
                └ JSONDecoder.decode   ← full ~800 KB catalog, per row
```

Because `refreshTokenUsage` runs when the menu is opened, this also presents as the menu **freezing on click**.

## Fix

Memoize the decoded artifact inside `ModelsDevCache`, keyed by the cache file's **path + size + modification date**:

- Repeated `load`s of an unchanged file reuse the in-memory `ModelsDevCatalog` instead of re-decoding, so the per-row fallback collapses to dictionary lookups.
- `isStale` still depends on `now`, so it is recomputed on every call — only the decoded artifact is cached.
- `save(...)` drops the memo entry so the next `load` picks up freshly written data; the size/mtime key also self-invalidates when the file changes underneath.
- Thread-safe via `NSLock` (cross-platform; the vendored CostUsage code also builds for Linux), mirroring the existing `PathEnvironment` / `ProviderStorageFootprint` cache helpers.

This is intentionally minimal and defensive: it does not change the threading of `modelsDevCatalog` through the scanner (which already exists), it just makes the nil-catalog fallback path cheap so a transient nil can no longer turn a full history scan into a CPU storm.

The codex parser hash (which covers files under `Vendored/CostUsage`) is regenerated; this also helps users by invalidating stale cost-usage caches so they rebuild on the fixed path.

## Tests

Added two cases to `ModelsDevPricingTests`:

- `serves decoded catalog from memo while the file is unchanged` — corrupts the cache bytes while preserving size + mtime and asserts the load still returns the originally decoded artifact (proving no re-decode).
- `saving a new catalog invalidates the memo` — asserts a subsequent load reflects freshly written data.

## Commands executed

```
swift build                              # Build complete
swift test --filter ModelsDevPricingTests  # 21 passed
swift test --filter CostUsage            # 163 passed
make format                              # swiftformat (pinned 0.59.1)
make check                               # swiftformat --lint + swiftlint --strict → 0 violations
./Scripts/regenerate-codex-parser-hash.sh
```

No UI changes, so no screenshots.

Refs #1311
